# TrueAsync

> Native asynchronous programming for PHP: coroutines, non-blocking I/O, structured concurrency, and channels — built into PHP core via the Zend Async API.

TrueAsync brings kernel-level asynchrony to PHP with zero-cost abstractions. Standard PHP functions (file I/O, network, PDO) work asynchronously inside coroutines without code changes. The project targets PHP 8.6 as an official extension.

Key concepts: Coroutine (lightweight concurrent unit), Scope (structured concurrency boundary), Future (deferred result), Channel (CSP-style communication), Context (hierarchical data propagation), Pool (resource management with circuit breaker).

## Getting Started

- [Documentation Hub](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs.html): Installation, learning resources, and organized guides
- [Introduction to Concurrency](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/introduction.html): PHP-FPM blocking model vs TrueAsync coroutine-based concurrency
- [Motivation](https://true-async.github.io/en/motivation.html): Why PHP needs built-in async and TrueAsync's approach
- [Download](https://true-async.github.io/en/download.html): Installation instructions

## Core Components

- [Coroutines](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/coroutines.html): Creation, lifecycle, state management, cancellation, error handling, and debugging
- [Scope](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/scope.html): Structured concurrency with hierarchical error handling and cancellation
- [TaskGroup](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/task-group.html): Execute and coordinate groups of tasks with waiting strategies (all, race, any) and concurrency limits
- [TaskSet](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/task-set.html): Dynamic task streams with automatic result cleanup and consumption-based iteration
- [Future](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/future.html): Deferred results with transformation chains via map, catch, finally
- [Channels](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/channels.html): CSP-style inter-coroutine communication with buffering and blocking
- [Context](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/context.html): Hierarchical execution data bound to Scopes without explicit parameter passing
- [Cancellation](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/cancellation.html): Cooperative cancellation, critical sections with protect(), cascading via Scope hierarchy
- [Exceptions](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/exceptions.html): Exception hierarchy — AsyncCancellation, TimeoutException, DeadlockError, CompositeException
- [Interfaces](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/interfaces.html): Base contracts — Awaitable, Completable, Timeout, ScopeProvider, SpawnStrategy
- [PDO Pool](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/pdo-pool.html): Transparent PDO connection pooling with auto transaction management and health checks
- [Pool](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/pool.html): Universal resource pool with acquire/release, health checking, and circuit breaker
- [Zombie Coroutines](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/zombie-coroutines.html): Fault-tolerance for unresponsive coroutines without blocking Scope termination
- [FileSystemWatcher](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/components/filesystem-watcher.html): Persistent file system monitoring with event buffering and coalescing

## API Reference — Functions

- [spawn()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/spawn.html): Launch a function in a new coroutine
- [spawn_with()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/spawn-with.html): Launch a coroutine in a specified Scope for explicit lifecycle control
- [await()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/await.html): Wait for a coroutine, Future, or Completable to complete
- [await_all()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/await-all.html): Wait for all tasks, collecting results and errors separately
- [await_all_or_fail()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/await-all-or-fail.html): Wait for all tasks or throw on first failure
- [await_any_of()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/await-any-of.html): Wait for first N tasks to complete
- [await_any_of_or_fail()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/await-any-of-or-fail.html): Wait for first N successes or throw on failure
- [await_any_or_fail()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/await-any-or-fail.html): Wait for first task, propagate its exception if thrown
- [await_first_success()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/await-first-success.html): Wait for first successful task
- [delay()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/delay.html): Suspend coroutine for specified milliseconds
- [suspend()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/suspend.html): Yield control to the scheduler
- [timeout()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/timeout.html): Create a timeout object for limiting wait times
- [protect()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/protect.html): Execute closure in non-cancellable mode
- [signal()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/signal.html): Wait for an OS signal, returns Future
- [iterate()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/iterate.html): Concurrent iteration with configurable concurrency
- [graceful_shutdown()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/graceful-shutdown.html): Initiate graceful scheduler shutdown
- [current_coroutine()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/current-coroutine.html): Get the currently executing coroutine object
- [current_context()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/current-context.html): Get Context bound to the current Scope
- [root_context()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/root-context.html): Get global root Context for the request
- [coroutine_context()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/coroutine-context.html): Get private Context of the current coroutine
- [get_coroutines()](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/get-coroutines.html): Get all active coroutines for diagnostics
- [Supported Functions](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/supported-functions.html): 70+ standard PHP functions adapted for non-blocking operation
- [INI Settings](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/ini-settings.html): Configuration directives for the TrueAsync extension

## API Reference — Classes

- [Coroutine](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/coroutine/get-id.html): getId, cancel, getState, getResult, isCancelled, isCompleted, and more
- [Scope](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/scope/construct.html): construct, spawn, cancel, getCoroutines, onException, and more
- [Future](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/future/construct.html): construct, completed, map, catch, finally, cancel, getResult, and more
- [Channel](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/channel/construct.html): construct, send, recv, sendAsync, recvAsync, close, getIterator, and more
- [Context](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/context/get.html): get, set, find, has, findLocal, getLocal, hasLocal, unset
- [TaskGroup](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/task-group/construct.html): construct, spawn, awaitAll, awaitRace, awaitAny, cancel, and more
- [TaskSet](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/task-set/construct.html): construct, spawn, join, joinNoFail, close, and more
- [Pool](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/pool/construct.html): construct, acquire, release, close, getStats, and more
- [FileSystemWatcher](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/filesystem-watcher/construct.html): construct, close, isClosed, getIterator

## Architecture

- [Architecture Overview](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture.html): Internal architecture, Zend Async ABI, scheduler-reactor design
- [Zend Async API](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/zend-async-api.html): ABI with function pointers, extension registration, global state
- [Scheduler & Reactor](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/scheduler-reactor.html): Context switching, microtasks, fiber pools, libuv event loop
- [Fibers in Async Mode](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/fibers.html): How PHP Fibers transform into coroutines when TrueAsync is active
- [Events](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/events.html): Universal zend_async_event_t structure — callbacks, lifecycle, event hierarchy
- [Waker](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/waker.html): Links coroutines to events with status tracking and result delivery
- [Async GC](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/async-gc.html): Garbage collection with separate GC coroutines for destructors that call await
- [PDO Pool Internals](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/pdo-pool.html): Two-level architecture, template connections, transaction pinning
- [Pool Internals](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/pool.html): Circular buffer, acquire/release algorithms, healthcheck timers, circuit breaker
- [FrankenPHP Integration](https://true-async.github.io/en/architecture/frankenphp.html): Request routing via Go channels, eventfd notification, scheduler heartbeat

## FrankenPHP

- [FrankenPHP Guide](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/frankenphp.html): Running TrueAsync with FrankenPHP — Docker, configuration, entrypoint, graceful restarts
- [Request](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/frankenphp/request.html): Read-only HTTP request data access
- [Response](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/frankenphp/response.html): Set status, headers, body, and send HTTP response
- [UploadedFile](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/reference/frankenphp/uploaded-file.html): Access uploaded file metadata and move to storage

## Optional

- [RFC](https://true-async.github.io/en/rfc.html): Two PHP RFCs proposing TrueAsync's concurrency model (draft status)
- [Contributing](https://true-async.github.io/en/contributing.html): How to contribute — code, docs, testing, community
- [Roadmap](https://true-async.github.io/en/roadmap.html): Development timeline — v0.7 Threads (Summer 2026), v1.0 target PHP 8.6 (November 2026)
- [Concurrency Efficiency Evidence](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/evidence/concurrency-efficiency.html): Benchmarks and performance data
- [Coroutines Evidence](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/evidence/coroutines-evidence.html): Research supporting coroutine model
- [Python Async Evidence](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/evidence/python-evidence.html): Lessons from Python's async ecosystem
- [Real-World Statistics](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/evidence/real-world-statistics.html): Production usage data
- [Swoole Evidence](https://true-async.github.io/en/docs/evidence/swoole-evidence.html): Comparison with Swoole's approach
